name: creating-client-singletons
author: djankies
description: Prevent multiple PrismaClient instances that exhaust connection pools causing P1017 errors. Use when creating PrismaClient, exporting database clients, setting up Prisma in new files, or encountering connection pool errors. Critical for serverless environments.

name: creating-custom-utilities
author: djankies
description: Create custom utilities with @utility directive supporting static utilities, functional utilities with values, theme-based utilities, and multi-value utilities. Use when extending Tailwind with custom CSS properties or patterns.

name: customizing-errors
author: djankies
description: Handle Zod validation errors with unified error API, custom messages, error formatting, and user-friendly display

name: deploying-production-migrations
author: djankies
description: Deploy migrations to production safely using migrate deploy in CI/CD. Use when setting up production deployment pipelines.

name: diagnosing-type-errors
author: djankies
description: Analyze TypeScript errors and provide detailed diagnostics with root cause analysis and specific fix recommendations

name: ensuring-query-type-safety
author: djankies
description: Use Prisma's generated types, `Prisma.validator`, and `GetPayload` for type-safe queries.
description_zh: 提供使用Prisma生成类型、Prisma.validator创建可复用片段以及GetPayload进行类型推断的具体模式。展示了关系查询、自定义选择和动态查询等常见场景的实用示例。
_original_description: Provides concrete patterns for type-safe database queries using Prisma's generated types, Prisma.validator for reusable fragments, and GetPayload for type inference. Shows practical examples for common scenarios like relations, custom selections, and dynamic queries.

name: following-the-rules-of-hooks
author: djankies
description: Fix React Rules of Hooks violations - conditional calls, hooks in loops/callbacks/classes

name: handling-transaction-errors
author: djankies
description: Handle transaction errors properly with P-code checking and timeout configuration. Use when implementing transaction error recovery.

name: implementing-code-splitting
author: djankies
description: Teaches code splitting with lazy() and Suspense in React 19 for reducing initial bundle size. Use when implementing lazy loading, route-based splitting, or optimizing performance.

name: implementing-optimistic-updates
author: djankies
description: Teaches useOptimistic hook for immediate UI updates during async operations in React 19. Use when implementing optimistic UI patterns, instant feedback, or reducing perceived latency.

name: implementing-query-caching
author: djankies
description: Implement query result caching with Redis and proper invalidation strategies for Prisma 6. Use when optimizing frequently accessed data, improving read-heavy application performance, or reducing database load through caching.

name: implementing-query-pagination
author: djankies
description: Implement cursor-based or offset pagination for Prisma queries. Use for datasets 100k+, APIs with page navigation, or infinite scroll/pagination mentions.

name: implementing-server-actions
author: djankies
description: Teaches Server Actions in React 19 for form handling and data mutations. Use when implementing forms, mutations, or server-side logic. Server Actions are async functions marked with 'use server'.

name: integrating-zod-frameworks
author: djankies
description: Integrate Zod v4 with React Hook Form, Next.js, Express, tRPC, and other frameworks for type-safe validation

name: managing-client-lifecycle
author: djankies
description: Manage PrismaClient lifecycle with graceful shutdown, proper disconnect timing, and logging configuration. Use when setting up application shutdown handlers, configuring logging for development or production, or implementing proper connection cleanup in Node.js servers, serverless functions, or test suites.

name: managing-dev-migrations
author: djankies
description: Use migrate dev for versioned migrations; db push for rapid prototyping. Use when developing schema changes locally.

name: managing-local-vs-global-state
author: djankies
description: Teaches when to use local state vs global state (Context) in React 19. Use when deciding state management strategy, avoiding prop drilling, or architecting component state.

name: managing-server-vs-client-boundaries
author: djankies
description: Teaches when to use Server Components vs Client Components in React 19, including the 'use client' directive and boundary patterns. Use when architecting components, choosing component types, or working with Server Components.

name: migrating-async-request-apis
author: djankies
description: Teach async request APIs in Next.js 16 - params, searchParams, cookies(), headers(), draftMode() are now async. Use when migrating from Next.js 15, fixing type errors, or working with request data.

name: migrating-from-forwardref
author: djankies
description: Teaches migration from forwardRef to ref-as-prop pattern in React 19. Use when seeing forwardRef usage, upgrading React components, or when refs are mentioned. forwardRef is deprecated in React 19.

name: migrating-from-v3
author: djankies
description: Migrate from Tailwind CSS v3 to v4 including configuration migration (JS to CSS), utility renames, opacity changes, and color system updates. Use when upgrading existing projects to v4.

name: migrating-middleware-to-proxy
author: djankies
description: Teach middleware.ts to proxy.ts migration in Next.js 16. Use when migrating middleware, encountering middleware errors, or implementing request proxying.

name: migrating-v3-to-v4
author: djankies
description: Complete migration guide from Zod v3 to v4 covering all breaking changes and upgrade patterns

name: optimizing-performance
author: djankies
description: Optimize Zod validation performance with safeParse, bulk array validation, schema reuse, and passthrough patterns

name: optimizing-query-performance
author: djankies
description: Optimize queries with indexes, batching, and efficient Prisma operations for production performance.

name: preloading-resources
author: djankies
description: Teaches resource preloading APIs in React 19 including prefetchDNS, preconnect, preload, and preinit. Use when optimizing initial load or navigation performance.

name: preventing-error-exposure
author: djankies
description: Prevent leaking database errors and P-codes to clients. Use when implementing API error handling or user-facing error messages.

name: preventing-sql-injection
author: djankies
description: Prevent SQL injection by using $queryRaw tagged templates instead of $queryRawUnsafe. Use when writing raw SQL queries or dynamic queries.

name: refactoring-inline-types
author: djankies
description: Refactor inline types into reusable, well-organized type definitions using interfaces, type aliases, and generics

name: resolving-type-errors
author: djankies
description: Resolve all TypeScript errors using root cause analysis, targeted fixes, and mandatory validation

name: reviewing-code-quality
author: djankies
description: Automated tooling and detection patterns for JavaScript/TypeScript code quality review

name: reviewing-complexity
author: djankies
description: Analyze code complexity and maintainability including cyclomatic complexity, function length, nesting depth, and cognitive load. Use when reviewing code maintainability, refactoring candidates, or technical debt assessment.

name: reviewing-component-architecture
author: djankies
description: Review component architecture for React 19 best practices including size, composition, Server/Client boundaries, and anti-patterns. Use when reviewing component design.

name: reviewing-dependencies
author: djankies
description: Automated tooling and detection patterns for analyzing npm dependencies, unused packages, and dead code. Provides tool commands and what to look for—not how to structure output.

name: reviewing-duplication
author: djankies
description: Automated tooling and detection patterns for identifying duplicate and copy-pasted code in JavaScript/TypeScript projects. Provides tool commands and refactoring patterns—not workflow or output formatting.

name: reviewing-hook-patterns
author: djankies
description: Review React hook usage for React 19 compliance and best practices

name: reviewing-patterns
author: djankies
description: Review Zod schemas for correctness, performance, and v4 best practices

name: reviewing-performance-patterns
author: djankies
description: Review React 19 performance patterns including memoization, re-renders, and bundle size. Use when reviewing performance or optimization.

name: reviewing-prisma-patterns
author: djankies
description: Review Prisma code for common violations, security issues, and performance anti-patterns found in AI coding agent stress testing. Use when reviewing Prisma Client usage, database operations, or performing code reviews on projects using Prisma ORM.

name: reviewing-security
author: djankies
description: Automated tooling and detection patterns for JavaScript/TypeScript security vulnerabilities. Provides scan commands, vulnerability patterns, and severity mapping—not output formatting or workflow.

name: reviewing-server-actions
author: djankies
description: Review Server Actions for security, validation, and best practices in React 19. Use when reviewing forms, mutations, or server-side logic.

name: reviewing-state-management
author: djankies
description: Review state management patterns for React 19 best practices. Use when reviewing component state, Context usage, or state architecture.

name: reviewing-test-quality
author: djankies
description: Review React 19 test quality including coverage, patterns, and React 19 API testing. Use when reviewing tests or test coverage.

name: reviewing-type-safety
author: djankies
description: Code review skill that checks TypeScript type safety, exported for use by cross-cutting review plugin

name: sanitizing-user-inputs
author: djankies
description: Sanitizing and validating user input to prevent XSS, injection attacks, and security vulnerabilities in TypeScript applications

name: securing-data-access-layer
author: djankies
description: Teach Data Access Layer pattern to prevent CVE-2025-29927 middleware authentication bypass. Use when implementing authentication, authorization, protecting routes, or working with server actions that need auth.

name: securing-server-actions
author: djankies
description: Teach server action authentication and security patterns in Next.js 16. Use when implementing server actions, form handlers, or mutations that need authentication.

name: supporting-custom-elements
author: djankies
description: Teaches Web Components (Custom Elements) support in React 19, including property vs attribute handling and custom events. Use when integrating Web Components or working with custom HTML elements.

name: testing-zod-schemas
author: djankies
description: Test Zod schemas comprehensively with unit tests, integration tests, and type tests for validation logic

name: transforming-string-methods
author: djankies
description: Write type-safe transformations with Zod including string methods, custom transforms, codecs, and pipelines

name: upgrading-to-prisma-6
author: djankies
description: Migrate from Prisma 5 to Prisma 6 handling breaking changes including Buffer to Uint8Array, implicit m-n PK changes, NotFoundError to P2025, and reserved keywords. Use when upgrading Prisma, encountering Prisma 6 type errors, or migrating legacy code.

name: using-action-state
author: djankies
description: Teaches useActionState hook for managing form state with Server Actions in React 19. Use when implementing forms, handling form submissions, tracking pending states, or working with Server Functions.

name: using-cache-directive
author: djankies
description: Teach Cache Components model with 'use cache' directive in Next.js 16. Use when implementing caching, optimizing performance, working with dynamic data, or migrating from Next.js 15 caching patterns.

name: using-cache-lifecycle-apis
author: djankies
description: Teach cache lifecycle APIs in Next.js 16 - cacheLife(), cacheTag(), updateTag(), refresh(), revalidateTag(). Use when managing cache invalidation, setting cache policies, or implementing cache tags.

name: using-container-queries
author: djankies
description: Use container queries for component-based responsive design with @container syntax and named containers. Use when building portable, reusable components that adapt to parent size instead of viewport.

name: using-context-api
author: djankies
description: Teaches Context API patterns in React 19 including use() hook for conditional context access. Use when implementing Context, avoiding prop drilling, or managing global state.

name: using-generics
author: djankies
description: Teaches generic constraints, avoiding any in generic defaults, and mapped types in TypeScript. Use when creating reusable functions, components, or types that work with multiple types while maintaining type safety.

name: using-interactive-transactions
author: djankies
description: Use interactive transactions with $transaction callback for atomic operations and automatic rollback. Use when operations must succeed or fail together.

name: using-runtime-checks
author: djankies
description: Teaches how to validate external data at runtime using Zod and other validation libraries in TypeScript. Use when working with APIs, JSON parsing, user input, or any external data source where runtime validation is needed.

name: using-the-use-hook
author: djankies
description: React 19's use() API for reading Promises/Context conditionally. For async data fetching, Suspense, conditional context access, useContext migration.

name: using-theme-variables
author: djankies
description: Define and use theme variables with @theme directive, oklch() color format, semantic naming, and namespaced utilities. Use when customizing design tokens or creating design systems.

name: using-type-guards
author: djankies
description: Teaches how to write custom type guards with type predicates and use built-in type narrowing in TypeScript. Use when working with unknown types, union types, validating external data, or implementing type-safe runtime checks.

name: validating-external-data
author: djankies
description: Validating external data from APIs, JSON parsing, user input, and any untrusted sources in TypeScript applications

name: validating-schema-basics
author: djankies
description: Validate code for Zod v4 compatibility by detecting deprecated APIs and ensuring adherence to v4 patterns

name: validating-string-formats
author: djankies
description: Guide to Zod v4 new features including top-level string formats, string transformations, codecs, and stringbool

name: validating-type-assertions
author: djankies
description: Teaches when type assertions are safe versus dangerous in TypeScript. Use when considering using 'as' keyword, type casting, or when working with external data that might use assertions instead of proper validation.

name: better-result-adopt
author: dmmulroy
description: Migrate codebase from try/catch or Promise-based error handling to better-result. Use when adopting Result types, converting thrown exceptions to typed errors, or refactoring existing error handling to railway-oriented programming.

name: better-result-migrate-v2
author: dmmulroy
description: Migrate better-result TaggedError from v1 (class-based) to v2 (factory-based) API

name: style-modeler
author: dongbeixiaohuo
description: 当用户需要学习某种风格、提取写作配方、建立风格库或模仿特定作者时调用。深度解构文本的15个维度，包括作者画像、思维内核、创作路径、互动设计等，建模为可精准复制的风格文件。触发词：风格建模、提取风格、学习风格、模仿写作、解构文章、写作配方、风格库。

name: web-article-extractor
author: dongbeixiaohuo
description: 使用 Chrome DevTools MCP 提取和分析网页文章内容。当用户请求获取网页内容、阅读在线文章、从网站提取文本、捕获网页快照或分析网页结构时使用。支持多种提取格式包括纯文本、HTML 和结构化内容。特别优化了微信公众号等有安全限制的网站。

name: workflow-producer
author: dongbeixiaohuo
description:
[MASTER ENTRY POINT] 写作工作流总导演 - 所有写作请求的唯一入口点。
触发词：帮我写、写文章、写一篇、创作、写作、产出、起草
第一步必须询问用户选择模式（A/B/C），禁止跳过，禁止自动判断。

name: link-analyzer
author: dragosroua
description: Comprehensive link analysis for static sites. Use when the user wants to analyze links, find broken links, identify orphan pages, detect under-linked or over-linked content, find link sinks, or understand their site's internal linking structure. Supports HTTP validation with false-positive filtering.

name: idea-mcp
author: dtprj
description: IntelliJ IDEA MCP for Java code analysis, testing, refactoring, and project navigation. Use when: (1) Checking Java compilation errors (get_file_problems is more accurate than LSP for multi-module Maven), (2) Running tests via IDEA run configurations, (3) Performing rename refactoring across the codebase, (4) Searching code or finding files in the project, (5) Executing terminal commands in IDEA context. Requires IDEA to be running with MCP plugin connected.
_original_description: IntelliJ-IDEA MCP provides powerful IDE features including running tests, code analysis, refactoring, search, and project navigation. Use this when you need accurate Java code analysis (avoiding LSP false positives), running tests via IDEA configurations, refactoring symbols, or exploring codebase structure. Key commands: execute_run_configuration (run tests), get_file_problems (accurate errors/warnings), search_in_files_by_text (search code), list_directory_tree (view structure), get_file_text_by_path (read files), rename_refactoring (safe refactoring), execute_terminal_command (run shell commands).

name: error-logger
author: dudusoar
description: Maintains detailed log of errors, bugs, and debugging sessions encountered during development. Use when encountering errors, debugging issues, or solving technical problems. Creates structured ERROR_LOG.md with error details, root causes, solutions, and prevention strategies. Complements project-logger (strategic decisions) with tactical debugging knowledge.

name: git-workflow
author: dudusoar
description: Quick reference for common Git commands and workflows. Use when working with version control, committing changes, managing branches, or resolving Git issues. Covers daily Git operations, branching strategies, and troubleshooting.

name: knowledge-extractor
author: dudusoar
description: Analyzes completed projects to identify generalizable patterns, common workflows, and reusable knowledge worth extracting into skills. Use after completing a project to review LOG.md, skill updates, and project artifacts. Generates extraction recommendations but does not modify skills directly (user decides whether to apply via skill-updater).

name: skill-contract-generator
author: dudusoar
description: Generates evolution contracts for skills, defining what can be updated, what must remain stable, and how to extract knowledge. Use when creating new skills or formalizing update rules for existing skills. Adds a "Skill Contract" section to SKILL.md that guides skill-updater during project work.

name: skill-updater
author: dudusoar
description: Updates skills during project work according to their contracts. Adds references, improves explanations, and refines examples while respecting skill boundaries. Use when discovering patterns, encountering edge cases, or finding better ways to explain skill concepts. Works with skills that have Skill Contracts defined by skill-contract-generator.

name: test-writer
author: dudusoar
description: Generates comprehensive test files for functions and classes. Use after writing a function, method, or class to quickly create unit tests with edge cases, assertions, and proper test structure. Supports multiple testing frameworks (pytest, unittest, jest, etc.) and follows testing best practices (AAA pattern, mocking, fixtures).
description_zh: 该技能为函数和类生成单元测试文件，支持多种测试框架。分析代码结构，识别包括边界条件在内的测试用例，按照AAA模式生成测试文件，包含正确的模拟和夹具。支持Python（pytest/unittest）和JavaScript（jest），工作流程文档清晰。
_original_description: This skill generates unit test files for functions and classes across multiple frameworks. It analyzes code structure, identifies test cases including edge conditions, and produces test files following AAA pattern with proper mocking and fixtures. Supports Python (pytest/unittest) and JavaScript (jest) with clear workflow documentation.

name: design-system-implementation
author: dundas
description: Build or update frontend components and pages that strictly adhere to the project design system defined in design/design-system.json (and design/design.json when present).
description_zh: 通过引用JSON文件中的设计令牌和模式，在构建UI组件时强制执行设计系统合规性。引导开发者使用现有模式而非临时样式，并提供了处理缺失系统或必要扩展的清晰工作流程。
_original_description: Enforces design system compliance when building UI components by referencing design tokens and patterns from JSON files. Guides developers to use existing patterns rather than creating ad-hoc styles, with clear workflows for handling missing systems or needed extensions.

name: dev-workflow-orchestrator
author: dundas
description: Orchestrate PRD creation, task generation, and gated task processing with user confirmations between phases.
description_zh: 编排三阶段开发流程：使用prd-writer创建需求文档，生成带父子结构的任务列表，并通过阶段确认门控的任务处理。强制结构化开发，在各阶段间设置审查暂停点。
_original_description: Orchestrates a three-phase development workflow: PRD creation using prd-writer, task generation with parent/sub-task breakdown, and gated task processing with user confirmations between phases. Enforces structured development with explicit pauses for review.

name: frontend-design-concept
author: dundas
description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
description_zh: 生成具有明确美学方向的前端代码，强制遵循特定设计原则以避免通用AI模式。在实现前要求明确概念选择，并提供排版、色彩、动效和布局的详细指导。
_original_description: Generates frontend code with strong aesthetic direction, enforcing specific design principles to avoid generic AI patterns. Provides detailed guidelines on typography, color, motion, and layout while requiring clear conceptual choices before implementation.

name: prd-writer
author: dundas
description: Create a clear, actionable PRD with clarifying questions and a junior-friendly structure.
description_zh: 根据功能需求提示生成结构化产品需求文档。先询问澄清问题，然后创建包含标准PRD章节的Markdown文件。为初级开发者提供清晰模板。
_original_description: Generates structured Product Requirements Documents from initial feature prompts. Asks clarifying questions first, then creates markdown files with standard PRD sections. Targets junior developers with clear templates.

name: task-processor
author: dundas
description: Process a task list one sub-task at a time with pause/confirm gates, test/commit protocol, and file tracking.
description_zh: 结构化任务处理器，将工作分解为需要手动确认的子任务。强制执行测试通过后才提交代码的流程。维护文件跟踪部分，并在步骤间需要用户批准。
_original_description: A structured task processor that breaks work into sub-tasks with manual confirmation gates. It enforces a test-commit protocol where changes are only committed after tests pass. Maintains a file tracking section and requires user approval between steps.

name: tasklist-generator
author: dundas
description: Generate high-level tasks and gated sub-tasks from a PRD, with relevant files and testing guidance.
description_zh: 将产品需求文档转换为结构化任务清单，包含相关文件引用和测试说明。采用两阶段流程，在高层任务和详细子任务之间需要用户确认。输出带编号检查清单的Markdown文件，并建议测试文件位置。
_original_description: Converts PRDs into structured task lists with file references and testing notes. Uses a two-phase approach with user confirmation between high-level tasks and detailed sub-tasks. Outputs markdown files with numbered checklists and co-located test file suggestions.

name: calendar
author: dvdsgl
description:
Calendar canvas for displaying events and picking meeting times.
Use when showing calendar views or when users need to select available time slots.
description_zh: 一个用于显示日历事件和交互式选择会议时间的画布技能。支持两种场景：只读日历显示和带多日历叠加的交互式会议时间选择器。用户可以浏览周视图、选择空闲时段并配置时间粒度。

name: canvas
author: dvdsgl
description:
**The primary skill for terminal TUI components.** Covers spawning, controlling, and interacting with terminal canvases.
Use when displaying calendars, documents, or flight bookings.

name: document
author: dvdsgl
description:
Document canvas for displaying and editing markdown content.
Use when showing documents, emails, or when users need to select text for editing.

name: flight
author: dvdsgl
description:
Flight canvas for comparing flights and selecting seats.
Use when users need to browse flight options and book seats.

name: architecture
author: dy9759
description: Comprehensive system architecture design and implementation workflow that orchestrates expert analysis, technical decision-making, and architectural pattern selection using the integrated toolset. Handles everything from initial system analysis to implementation-ready technical specifications.

name: code-test-review-expert
author: dy9759
description: Advanced code testing and review expert system that provides comprehensive code quality analysis, security vulnerability assessment, test strategy design, and quality assurance through multi-expert collaboration and intelligent tool integration.

name: context-engineering-expert
author: dy9759
description: Advanced context engineering management system that provides comprehensive context architecture design, memory management, knowledge engineering, and workflow orchestration through expert collaboration and intelligent tool integration.

name: frontend-web-dev-expert
author: dy9759
description: Advanced frontend web development expert system that provides comprehensive modern web development services including architecture design, UI/UX implementation, performance optimization, engineering setup, and cross-platform development through expert collaboration and intelligent tool integration.

name: prd
author: dy9759
description: Complete PRD (Product Requirements Document) creation workflow that guides users from initial idea to implementation-ready specification using the integrated toolset in this repository. Handles everything from brainstorming and market analysis to technical requirements and team coordination.

name: prompt-engineer
author: dy9759
description: Comprehensive LLM prompt engineering and optimization workflow that orchestrates expert analysis, advanced techniques, and multi-domain optimization using the integrated toolset. Handles everything from basic prompt improvement to complex multi-agent prompt orchestration.

name: skill-forge
author: dy9759
description: Meta-skill creation and optimization system that analyzes, designs, and builds new specialized skills using the integrated toolset. Transforms skill creation from art to science through pattern recognition, intelligent tool selection, and automated quality assurance.
description_zh: 一个具有创新性的技能创建元框架，概念设计出色，但缺乏具体的实现细节和工具规范。
_original_description: An ambitious meta-skill framework for systematic skill creation with strong conceptual innovation, but lacking concrete implementation details and tool specifications.

name: dyad-multi-pr-review
author: dyad-sh
description: Multi-agent code review system that spawns three independent Claude sub-agents to review PR diffs. Each agent receives files in different randomized order to reduce ordering bias. One agent focuses specifically on code health and maintainability. Issues are validated using reasoned analysis rather than simple vote counting. Reports merge verdict (YES / NOT SURE / NO). Automatically deduplicates against existing PR comments. Always posts a summary (even if no new issues), with low priority issues in a collapsible section.

name: dyad-swarm-pr-review
author: dyad-sh
description: Team-based PR review using Claude Code swarm. Spawns three specialized teammates (correctness expert, code health expert, UX wizard) who review the PR diff, discuss findings with each other, and reach consensus on real issues. Posts a summary with merge verdict and inline comments for HIGH/MEDIUM issues.

name: base64-parser
author: echoVic
description: Base64 编解码工具，支持编码/解码文本、JSON、二进制文件，自动检测格式，支持批量处理

name: code-review
author: echoVic
description: Review frontend code for best practices, bugs, and improvements. Use when reviewing PRs, checking code quality, or before committing.
